subject: Geography
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the concept of water as a renewable resource
Explain the causes and effects of water scarcity
Understand the importance of water conservation
Describe multipurpose river projects and their functions
Evaluate advantages and disadvantages of dams
Learn traditional and modern methods of rainwater harvesting
Develop awareness about sustainable water management

lesson_plan.title: Water as a Renewable Resource
teaching_strategy: Diagram explanation of the hydrological cycle

lesson_plan.title: Water Scarcity
topics_covered: Effects of population growth and industrialization
student_activities: Identify causes of water scarcity in their locality
Write short notes on water scarcity

lesson_plan.title: Need for Water Conservation
student_activities: Suggest methods to conserve water at home
Prepare a list of water-saving practices

lesson_plan.title: Multipurpose River Projects
topics_covered: Examples such as Bhakra Nangal and Hirakud
student_activities: Locate major dams on a map of India
List uses of multipurpose river projects

lesson_plan.title: Advantages and Disadvantages of Dams
student_activities: Create a comparison table of advantages and disadvantages

lesson_plan.title: Rainwater Harvesting
topics_covered: Traditional systems like tankas and johads
teaching_strategy: Example from different regions of India
student_activities: Draw a rooftop rainwater harvesting system

lesson_plan.title: Traditional Irrigation Systems and Revision
student_activities: Explain how bamboo drip irrigation works

teaching_aids: Charts showing hydrological cycle and rainwater harvesting
teacher_tip: Use local examples of water usage and conservation, and encourage students to observe water-saving practices in their homes and communities.
